title: New Multimedia App: MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera

requirements.text: Create a new standalone React application (separate from the existing conversion tool) at the root level with a tabbed navigation layout containing four tabs: 'MP3 Cutter', 'Video Cutter', 'Video Downloader', and 'IP Camera'. Include a dark mode / light mode toggle in the header. The layout must be fully mobile-responsive with smooth tab-switching animations.

quotes: create a new multimedia app there are option MP3 cutter, video cutter, video downloder, IP Camera

acceptanceCriteria: App renders with four clearly labeled tabs: MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera
Dark/light mode toggle is present in the header and switches the theme
Tab switching is smooth with animation
Layout is fully mobile-responsive on screens 320px and wider
Active tab is visually highlighted

requirements.text: Implement the MP3 Cutter module. Provide a file upload button that accepts MP3, AAC, WAV, and MP4 files. After upload, load the audio using the Web Audio API and render a waveform visualization on a Canvas element. Display the audio duration. Allow the user to drag two range handles (start and end markers) on the waveform to select a segment of the audio track. Show the selected start time and end time numerically. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that, when clicked, extracts the selected audio segment client-side (using AudioBuffer slicing via the Web Audio API and encoding to MP3 using a CDN-loaded lamejs or similar encoder library), then triggers a browser download of the trimmed audio as an MP3 file.

quotes: MP3 cutter: import mp3, AAC, WAV, mp5 or mp4 file convert in mp3 audio cutter, user select audio track and cut and dounload audio file

acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts .mp3, .aac, .wav, .mp4 files
Waveform is rendered on a Canvas after file is loaded
Two draggable handles allow selecting start and end positions on the waveform
Selected start time and end time are displayed numerically in seconds or MM:SS format
Clicking 'Cut & Download' triggers a browser download of the trimmed audio as an MP3 file
The downloaded file contains only the audio between the selected start and end markers

requirements.text: Implement the Video Cutter module. Provide a file upload button that accepts all common video formats (MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, MKV). After upload, display the video in an HTML5 <video> element for playback preview. Render a timeline scrubber with two draggable handles (start and end trim points) so the user can define the clip region. Show the selected start and end times numerically. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that uses the MediaRecorder API or ffmpeg.wasm (loaded lazily from CDN) to extract and encode the selected video segment, then triggers a browser download of the trimmed video file. Show a loading/progress indicator during processing.

quotes: video cutter: all video file import and user crop video and cut by button

acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, and MKV files
Uploaded video is shown in a playback preview element
Timeline scrubber with two draggable trim handles is displayed below the video
Start and end time values update in real time as handles are dragged
Clicking 'Cut' processes the video and downloads the trimmed segment
A progress/loading indicator is shown during processing
Downloaded file contains only the selected clip

requirements.text: Implement the Video Downloader module. Provide a URL input field where users paste a YouTube video link. Provide a 'Download' button that, when clicked, calls the YouTube Data API v3 using the API key AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ to fetch video metadata (title, thumbnail). Display the video thumbnail and title. Since direct binary download of YouTube videos is blocked by CORS, the 'Download' button must open the YouTube watch URL in a new browser tab and display a clear user-facing message: 'The video will open in a new tab — use your browser or a download extension to save it.' Show a loading spinner while fetching metadata and a user-friendly error message if the URL is invalid or the API returns an error.

quotes: video downloder: user copy youtube link for download video by one click download button

acceptanceCriteria: URL input field accepts a pasted YouTube link
Clicking 'Download' fetches metadata from YouTube Data API v3 using the provided API key
Video thumbnail and title are displayed after metadata is fetched
Download button opens the YouTube URL in a new tab
A clear message informs the user the video will open in a new tab for saving
Loading spinner is shown while fetching metadata
A clear error message is displayed if the URL is invalid or the API call fails

requirements.text: Implement the IP Camera module. Provide an 'IP Cam' button that, when clicked, requests camera access via navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia (video: true) and streams the live video feed into an HTML5 <video> element displayed within the panel. Simultaneously, detect the device's local WiFi IP address by fetching from a public STUN-based WebRTC ICE candidate gathering approach (using RTCPeerConnection with a STUN server to extract the local IP from ICE candidates). Display the detected local IP address prominently (e.g., 'Access camera at: http://<local-ip>:PORT') along with a note that any browser on the same WiFi network can use that address to view the stream. Display a clear error message if camera access is denied. Show a 'Stop Camera' button to stop the stream and release the camera.

quotes: IP Camera: user click Ip Cam button camera start video capturing and showing internet wifi ip address to connect any browser for access camra view

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'IP Cam' button requests camera permission and starts the live video stream in the panel
Live video feed is displayed in a <video> element
The device's local WiFi IP address is detected and displayed after the camera starts
The displayed address is in the format 'http://<local-ip>' so the user can share it
A note explains that devices on the same network can use this address to view the stream
A 'Stop Camera' button stops the stream and clears the video feed
A clear error message is shown if camera permission is denied

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark-first visual design system to the entire Multimedia App. Use deep charcoal/slate backgrounds, vibrant teal/cyan accent colors for buttons and active states, crisp white typography, subtle glassmorphism card surfaces (backdrop-filter blur with semi-transparent backgrounds), and smooth micro-interaction animations (button hover states, tab transitions, progress indicators). Enforce this design system consistently across all four module tabs (MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera). Reuse the glass-card and tool-btn CSS utility class patterns consistent with the existing app's index.css.
acceptanceCriteria: All four module tabs use deep charcoal/slate backgrounds
Teal/cyan accent colors are applied to primary buttons and active tab indicators
Card surfaces use glassmorphism styling (backdrop blur, semi-transparent background)
Typography is crisp white on dark backgrounds
Button hover states and tab transitions have smooth animations
Design is consistent across all four modules

constraints: All audio and video processing must be client-side only — no backend storage or processing
The IP Camera feature can only display the local IP address via WebRTC ICE candidate extraction; it cannot act as a true broadcast server from the browser context alone
Direct YouTube video binary download is blocked by CORS — the downloader must open the URL in a new tab instead
ffmpeg.wasm or audio encoding libraries must be lazy-loaded from CDN to avoid large initial bundle sizes
The backend Motoko actor must not be modified — it remains a minimal getAppInfo endpoint
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Actual server-side video streaming or WebRTC signaling server for IP Camera
True direct YouTube or Facebook video binary download (blocked by CORS and platform ToS)
Modifying the existing conversion tool app (Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter, Passport Photo, BG Remover)
User authentication or cloud storage of media files
